sections.heading: EU Customs Reform 2026: End of the €150 Duty Exemption for Low-Value Imports
sections.text: Blog > EU Customs Reform 2026: End of the €150 Duty Exemption for Low-Value Imports EU Customs Reform 2026: End of the €150 Duty Exemption for Low-Value Imports by: Mila Jun 24,2026 1694 Views 0 Comments Posted in News The new EU Customs Reform (IOSS 2.0) will officially take effect on July 1, 2026. Starting from July 1, 2026, the European Union will abolish the €150 customs duty exemption for imported goods. Low-value shipments will be subject to a €3 customs duty per declaration item and must comply with newly introduced product data declaration requirements. According to the EU Customs Reform proposal: 1. Implementation Date and Scope Target implementation date: July 1, 2026 Scope of app...

sections.heading: 1. Implementation Date and Scope
sections.text: The new EU Customs Reform (IOSS 2.0) will officially take effect on July 1, 2026. Starting from July 1, 2026, the European Union will abolish the €150 customs duty exemption for imported goods. Low-value shipments will be subject to a €3 customs duty per declaration item and must comply with newly introduced product data declaration requirements. According to the EU Customs Reform proposal: 1. Implementation Date and Scope Target implementation date: July 1, 2026 Scope of application: Low-value goods (≤ €150) imported from non-EU countries into any of the 27 EU member states, including both B2C and B2B transactions. 2. Online Sellers Become Importers Starting July 1, 2026, all cross-border...
